original: Battle of St Matthews Day: German forces defeat Estonian army and kill their leader Lembitu, during Northern Crusades to Christianize the north
Jews in Zurich, Switzerland, are accused of poisoning wells
Portuguese, led by Henry the Navigator, assault Moroccan city of Tangiers, later ends in disaster when they are surrounded by the army of vizier Abu Zakariya Yahya al-Wattasi of Fez
Cardinal Nicholas of Cusa orders Jews of Holland to wear a badge
Shrine of Saint Swithun in Winchester smashed up in the middle of the night as part of the English Reformation
French bishops recognizeHenry IVas King of France
Beginning of the Battle at Pilawce: Bohdan Khmelnytsky's Cossacks defeat the Polish army of John Casimir II (battle ends 23 Sept) [NS]
Benedetto Odescalchi elected as Pope Innocent XI
Jan and Nicolaas van der Heyden patent the fire hose
Battle of Preston Pans: Bonnie Prince Charles beats British government army
French expeditionary army occupies Labourdonnais and Dupleix Madras
Antoine de Beauterne announces he has killed the Beast of Gévaudan, but was later proved wrong by more attacks
5 days after British take New York, a quarter of the city burns down
Pennsylvania Packet and Daily Advertiser becomes the 1st successful daily newspaper in the United States
Inauguration of King Willem I of the Netherlands in Brussels
According toJoseph SmithJr., the angel Moroni gave him a record of gold plates, one-third of which Joseph translated into The Book of Mormon
In the Second Opium War, an Anglo-French force defeats Chinese troops at the Battle of Baliqiao
Union forces retreat to Chattanooga after defeat at Chickamauga
John Henry Conyers of South Carolina becomes first black student at Annapolis
First direct US-Brazil telegraph connection
Dutch demonstrate for general voting rights
Frank Duryea drives the 1st American-made gas propelled vehicle
America's first automotive producer, the Duryea Motor Wagon Company, is founded by Charles and J. Frank Duryea
NY Sun runs famous "Yes, Virginia there is a Santa Claus" editorial
First cowboy film "Kit Carson" premieres in US
The general strike called by the Socialist Party that spread throughout Italy ends
Atlanta Life Insurance Co forms
New York Highlanders 1st baseman Hal Chase has 22 put-outs to tie MLB record in a 6-3 win over Chicago White Sox at South Side Park
France's Adolphe Pégoud becomes the second pilot to fly a loop
Turkey and Bulgaria sign peace treaty in Constantinople
Emanuel Querido ("Kerido") begins publishing Querido
A storage silo at a BASF fertiliser producing plant explodes in Oppau, Germany, 500—600 killed
Pope Benedictus XV donates 1 million lire to feed Russians
First issue of the magazine "My Weekly Reader" is published
First legal pass in Canada is thrown by Gerry Seiberling, and first reception is made by Ralph Losie of the Calgary Altomah-Tigers against Edmonton
Johann Ostermeyer patents flashbulb
Britain abandons gold standard; pound devalues 20%
Trial against Marinus der Lubbe opens
St Louis Cardinals pitching Dean brothers shut-out Brooklyn Dodgers in a doubleheader; Dizzy, in a 13-0 rout and Paul, with a 3-0 no-hitter
Typhoon strikes Honshu Island Japan, kills 4,000
RKO film comedy "Room Service", starring the Marx Brothers, based on Allen Boretz and John Murray's play, premieres in NYC
The Great Hurricane of 1938 makes landfall on Long Island in New York, killing an estimated 500-700 people
116 hostages executed by Nazis in Paris
Transport #35 departs with French Jews to nazi-Germany
Japanese troops withdrawal from Arundel Island in the Solomon Islands
Lynch Triangle (Square) in Bronx named
Soviet 13th/61st Army reconquer Chyernigov
Soviet forces reach Dnjepr
Operation Market Garden: Last British paratroopers at Arnhem Bridge surrender after several days of fighting
Operation Market Garden: Polish paratroopers land at Driel after a delay due to bad weather and a shortage of planes
Indians play their final game in League Park, ending a 55-year stay
George Marshall sworn in as the 3rd Secretary of Defense of the United States

MLB Boston Braves play what turns out to be their last home game at Braves Field in Boston, losing to the Brooklyn Dodgers 8–2 before crowd of 8,822; team relocates to Milwaukee before start of next season
KRDO TV channel 13 in Colorado Spgs-Pueblo, CO (ABC) 1st broadcast
Eelco Van Kleffens appointed President of the ninth session of the UN General Assembly
Last allied occupying troops leave Austria
USSR performs nuclear test
German sail training ship Pamir sails Atlantic Ocean
Olav V becomes king of Norway upon the death of his father Haakon VII
Pote Sarasin forms government in Thailand

American pilots Jim Heth and Bill Burkhart complete 1st airplane flight exceeding 1200 hours (50 days, 16 minutes), landing "The Old Scotchman", their modified Cessna 172 in Dallas, Texas; record broken 123 days later
US performs nuclear test at Nevada Test Site
600 Indian Dutch emigrate to US
Antonio Abertondo swims English Channel both ways (44 miles)
Maiden flight of the CH-47 Chinook military transport helicopter
Malta gains independence from the United Kingdom
The North American XB-70 Valkyrie, the world's first Mach 3 bomber, makes its maiden flight from Palmdale, California
O Kommissarova (USSR) sets women's longest parachute jump (46,250')
Singapore admitted as part of the United Nations.
Five inches of rain falls on NYC
US performs nuclear test at Nevada Test Site
Police drama "Adam-12" debuts on NBC in the US
Soviet spacecraft Zond 5 lands in the Indian ocean after a six-day flight - first successful circumlunar mission with two onboard tortoises surviving the trip
AFL New York Jets Steve O'Neal (23) kicks 98 yard punt, the longest professional American football punt in Denver against the Broncos
NY Jet Steve O'Neal punts 98 yards against Denver Broncos
Ron Hill wins European marathon (2:16:47.8)
KAPP TV channel 35 in Yakima, WA (ABC) begins broadcasting
New York Times starts first modern op-ed page
Soviet space probe Luna 16 leaves the Moon
AL OKs Washington Senator move to Arlington (Texas Rangers)
USSR performs underground nuclear test
NY Mets go into 1st place (at .500) after trailing 12½ games
US Mariner 10 makes 2nd fly-by of Mercury
Wings performs in Zagreb Yugoslavia
The Provisional Irish Republican Army explodes bombs at the RAF airfield near Eglinton, County Londonderry; the terminal building, two aircraft hangars and four planes are destroyed
José Eduardo dos Santos becomes President of Angola

Two RAF Hawker Siddeley Harrier jump-jets from RAF Wittering collide over Wisbech in Cambridgeshire. Both pilots eject safely, but three people killed and several injured when one of the aircraft destroys 3 dwellings
Kerry GAA beat Roscommon GAA in Croke Park during the All-Ireland Football Final by 1-9 to 1-6 thus winning the championship and a three-in-a-row.
LA Ram Johnnie Johnson scores a 99 yard interception
Richard Todd of NY Jets completes 42 passes in a game (NFL record)
Belize (British Honduras) gains independence from the United Kingdom
Enterprise Radio (all sports) goes off the air
US Senate confirms Sandra Day O'Conner to Supreme Court (99-0)
2,251 turn out to see Expos play NY Mets at Shea Stadium
NFL players begin a 57-day strike
San Francisco cable cars cease operations for 2 years of repairs
STS-5 vehicle moves to launch pad
11 killed in anti Marcos demonstrations in Manila
US performs nuclear test at Nevada Test Site
NASA launches Galaxy-C
Kerry GAA beat Tyrone GAA in Croke Park during the All-Ireland Football Final by 2-15 to 1-10 thus winning the championship and a three-in-a-row.
New Orleans Saints Mel Gray returns kickoff 101 yards for a touchdown
NY Jets beat Miami Dolphins 51-45 in OT; record 884 passing yards
SD Padre Jimmy Jones pitches 1-hitter in his major league debut
3 Belgian minesweepers depart to Persians Gulf
East German swimmer Silke Hörner sets world record 2:26.71 to win the 200m breaststroke gold medal at the Seoul Olympics; first of 2 gold (4 x 100m medley relay)
Hungarian swimmer Tamás Darnyi sets world record 4:14.75 to win the 400m individual medley at the Seoul Olympics; also wins 200m I/M gold for the medley double
Poland's Sejm (National Assembly) approves prime minister Mazowiecki
MLB Commissioner Faye Vincent turns down Chicago White Sox bid to reinstate 68-year-old Minnie Miñoso
Oakland A's Bob Welch becomes 1st 25 game winner in 10 years
Pirate Bobby Bond is 2nd to hit 30 HRs & steal 50 bases in a season (so he can play in 6 decades) because it is a publicity stunt
Armenia votes on whether to remain in Soviet Union
USA Basketball announces "Dream Team" for the 1992 Olympics
Ukraine government of Kutshma resigns
Howard Stern Radio Show premieres in Ft Lauderdale and Miami Floroda on WBGG 105.9 FM
The Hindu milk miracle occurs: statues of the Hindu God Ganesh begin drinking milk when spoonfuls were placed near their mouths
Mike Piazza is 2nd to hit a HR out of Dodger Stadium
TV comedy "Will & Grace" starring Eric McCormack and Debra Messing premieres in the US
Chi-Chi earthquake occurs in central Taiwan, leaving about 2,400 people dead
AZF chemical plant explodes in Toulouse, France, killing 31 people
Deep Space 1 flies within 2,200 km of Comet Borrelly.
University of Roorkee becomes India's 7th Indian Institute of Technology, rechristened as IIT Roorkee
Galileo mission terminated by sending the probe into Jupiter's atmosphere, where it is crushed by the pressure at the lower altitudes
Communist Party of India (Marxist-Leninist) People's War and the Maoist Communist Centre of India merge to form the Communist Party of India (Maoist)
Construction of the Burj Dubai starts
Final home game is played at the original Yankee Stadium against the Baltimore Orioles
Last two remaining independent investment banks on Wall Street, Goldman Sachs and Morgan Stanley, become bank holding companies as a result of the subprime mortgage crisis
Prime Minister of Israel Ehud Olmert resigns from office, effective when his successor Tzipi Livni forms a new government
Macedonia officially enters the recession after experiencing a drop in GDP of 0.9% in the first quarter, followed by 1.4% in the second quarter
Scandinavian crime drama "The Bridge" created by Hans Rosenfeldt, starring Sofia Helin and Kim Bodnia premieres
Japanese Prime Minister, Yoshihiko Noda, wins a leadership ballot
100 people are killed in a series of attacks across Iraq
59 people are killed and 175 wounded in a shopping mall gun battle in Nairobi, Kenya
6 people are killed in military and insurgent conflict in Zamboanga City, Philippines
Scott Walker, Governor of Wisconsin, pulls out of the Republican US Presidential race
Genomic study finding Australian Aboriginal oldest known civilisation on earth published in "Nature"
Migrant boat carrying 450-600 people capsizes off the Egyptian coast, only 163 rescued
Three genetic studies published in "Nature" conclude all non-Africans descended from one migration out of Africa 50-80,000 years ago
Andre Ward, unified light heavyweight champion and world’s top pound-for-pound boxer, announces his retirement
Fossil of Dickinsonia, “the Holy Grail of palaeontology” proven to be oldest known animal fossil, 558 million years old from White Sea, Russia
"Queer Eye" star Jonathan Van Ness reveals he is living with HIV and is a sexual abuse survivor, ahead of the publication of his autobiography
Skies turn red over Jambi province, Indonesia, as worst illegal forest fires since 2015 burn more than 800,000 acres, creating respiratory problems for a million people
Canadian province of Quebec expands restrictions, declaring it is in a second COVID-19 wave as cases across the country average 1,000 a day
McDonald's announces plans to "drastically" reduce plastic in its Happy Meals by 2025 (these meals make it one of largest toy distributors in the world)
Russian authorities claim 5,397 Russian soldiers have died fighting in Ukraine, Westerns sources put the figure at 25,000 to over 80,000 (NY Times)
An explosion at a coal mine in Tabas, eastern Iran, kills at least 50 people and leaves others trapped underground
Google signs a 20-year deal to buy power from the Three Mile nuclear power plant, the site of a nuclear accident in 1979
Julius Caesarcelebrates first of four triumphal processions in Rome - over Gaul, Egypt, Pontus and Africa with leader of the Gauls Vercingetorix led in chains
English KingRichard Ithe Lion hearted, captured by Leopold V, Duke of Austria
Treaty of Arras (1435) signed between Charles VII of France andPhilip the Goodof Burgundy
James Vcrowned King of Scotland in the Chapel Royal at Stirling Castle, aged 17 months
Battle at Arques: French KingHenry IVbeats Catholic League
King James Iof England gives Scottish courtier and poet William Alexander a royal charter to colonize Nova Scotia
Nathan Hale, American rebel spy is arrested by British militia in NYC
Benedict Arnoldgives British Major John André plans to West Point
French Revolution: The National Convention passes a proclamation announcing the formal abolition of the French monarchy
French National Convention orders the remains ofComte de Mirabeaube removed from the Panthéon after his double-dealings with court revealed
Jean-Paul Marat's remains are transferred to the Panthéon in Paris, with the eulogy given by theMarquis de Sade
Moroni first appears toJoseph Smith, according to Smith
BritishGeneral Kitchener's army occupies Dongola, Sudan
Empress Dowager Cixiseizes power and ends the Hundred Days' Reform in China, imprisoning the Guangxu Emperor
Canadian Prime MinisterWilfrid Laurierloses the election toRobert Bordenof the Conservative Party
US PresidentWarren G. Hardingsigns a joint resolution of approval to establish a Jewish homeland in Palestine

Wallal expedition of astronomers and local Nyangumarta people south of Broome, Australia, photograph total solar eclipse provingAlbert Einstein's Theory of Relativity, that space is curved by showing deflection of starlight round the sun
Spanish fascist junta namesFrancisco FrancoGeneralissimo and Supreme Commander
J. R. R. Tolkien's "The Hobbit" is published by George Allen and Unwin in London
British politicianWinston Churchillcondemns Germany'sAdolf Hitler's annexation of Czechoslovakia
Nazi Gestapo leaderReinhard Heydrichmeets in Berlin to discuss final solution of Jews
Milton Berlebecomes the permanent host on NBC's TV show "Texaco Star Theater"
Mao Zedongand Chinese Communist leaders proclaim the People's Republic of China
Emil Zatopek runs 15,000m in record 44 min, 54.6 sec
In his last fight, undefeated world heavyweight boxing championRocky MarcianoKOs light heavyweightArchie Moorein the 9th round at Yankee Stadium, NYC
New York Yankees set dubious MLB record, stranding 20 on base;Mickey Mantlehits a 500' plus homer but rival Boston Red Sox win 13-9 at Fenway Park
"Perry Mason" TV series starring Raymond Burr, based on the character by authorErle Stanley Gardner, premieres on CBS-TV
Jimmy Hendrixchanges spelling of his name to "Jimi"
"Monday Night Football" created byRoone Arledge, premieres on ABC - Browns 31, Jets 21
Oakland A'sVida Blueno-hits Minnesota Twins, 6-0
Philippines PresidentFerdinand Marcosdeclares martial law in the Philippines (not publicly announced until 23rd Sep)
Jackson Pollocks painting "Blue Poles" sold for $2,000,000
Nate Archibaldsigns 7 yr contract with NBA KC Kings for $450,000
Enzo Maiorcasets the world record for free diving of 90 metres

Asylum Records releases "Small Change", the fourth studio album by singer-songwriterTom Waits; his best-selling album until 1999, features the songs "Tom Traubert's Blue" and "The Piano Has Been Drinking (Not Me)"
Orlando Letelier is assassinated in Washington, D.C. He was a member of the Chilean socialist government of Salvador Allende, overthrown in 1973 byAugusto Pinochet.

Stephen Jay Gouldand Richard Lewontin publish influential paper (cited more than 4,000 times) "The Spandrels of San Marco and the panglossian paradigm", introducing idea of "spandrels" into evolutionary biology
Reggae singerBob Marleycollapses while jogging in New York City's Central Park; medical exam finds his cancer had spread to his brain, lungs, and liver
MLB Philadelphia Phillies lefthanderSteve Carltonstrikes out NL record 3,118th (Andre Dawson)
David Mamet's play "Glengarry Glen Ross" premieres in London
American CIA case officerEdward Lee Howardflees to Russia after being identified as a KGB agent
Michael Spinks beatsLarry Holmesin 15 to become Heavyweight Boxing Champion
38th Emmy Awards: "The Golden Girls", "Cagney & Lacey" andMichael J. Foxwin
Mike Tysonsmashes TV camera outside his Bernardsville, New Jersey home
Suriname swimmer Anthony Nesty wins 100m butterfly at the Seoul Olympics; Suriname's first gold; first black to win individual Olympic swim gold medal; thwartsMatt Biondi's attempt at 7 gold medals
Reports that US refinery problems will lead to a loss in capacity and aggressive remarks bySaddam Husseinsend crude prices to new highs
Melissa Etheridgereleases her breakthrough second album "Yes I Am", featuring the single "I'm the Only One"
NY YankeeCecil Fielderhits his 300th HR
55th Emmy Awards:The West Wing, Everybody Loves Raymond,James Gandolfini&Edie Falcowins
Green Dayrelease their album "American Idiot" in the US
"Into the Wild" starringEmile Hirschis released
George Clooneyis injured in a motorcycle accident in Weehawken, New Jersey
60th Emmy Awards: Mad Men, 30 Rock,Bryan Cranston&Glenn Closewin
PresidentThabo Mbekiof South Africa resigns from office, effective September 25
67th Emmy Awards: hosted by Andy Samberg, "Game of Thrones", Viola Davis, John Hamm win
Facebook CEOMark Zuckerbergand his wife Priscilla Chan pledge $3 billion to medical research to "cure, prevent or manage all diseases by the end of the century"
American singer-songwriterPaul Simoncloses his "Homeward Bound – The Farewell Tour" at Flushing Meadows Corona Park in Queens, NYC
WHO DirectorTedros Ghebreyesussays countries representing 2/3 of the world's population have joined its COVAX vaccine-distribution initiative to deliver 2 billion doses by end 2021
World leaders address climate change at the UN,Joe Bidenpledges to double financial aid to developing countries, PresidentXi Jinpingsays China will stop coal-fired projects abroad
New York Attorney General Letitia James files lawsuit againstDonald Trumpand three of his children, alleging widespread fraud through inflating his personal net worth by billions of dollars
Vladimir Putinannounces partial mobilization of Russian population, drafting between 300,000 and 1.2m men to fight in Russia's invasion of Ukraine, prompting demonstrations around the country

KingCharles IIIbecomes the first British monarch to make a speech from France’s senate chamber on a three day trip to the country

Discovery of the first brainless animal that sleeps, the jellyfish Cassiopea, research published in "Current Biology" by Caltech scientists
